sections.title: Falsification Court
sections.text:
Adversarial inspection of break conditions: what fails when core claims are denied.
::::
:::::: badge-row
::: badge

sections.title: Reject external grace
sections.text:
The works-based sign-flip becomes impossible under the framework's non-unitary requirement.
::::
:::: {.node .derivation .dead}
::: tag

sections.title: Reject information primacy
sections.text:
The system falls back into circular definitions or regress because rival primitives borrow informational structure.
::::
:::: {.node .derivation .dead}
::: tag

sections.title: Reject Time Wall
sections.text:
Intentional incompleteness is denied, but the unresolved boundary problem does not disappear; it is merely hidden.
::::
:::: {.node .derivation .dead}
::: tag

sections.title: Reject terminal observer
sections.text:
The measurement / interpretive chain remains open and the closure claim is never completed.
::::
:::: {.node .derivation .dead}
::: tag

sections.title: Reject coherence conservation
Court function
sections.text:
This page is adversarial by design. It asks what happens when core claims are denied. The answer is not "nothing." The answer is specific structural damage.

claims.one_sentence_claim: The system falls back into circular definitions or regress because rival primitives borrow informational structure. :::: :::: {.node .derivation .dead} ::: tag
claims.facts_snapshot: No hard factual terms detected by deterministic pass.
claims.forward_test: If the claim is true, the described pattern should appear where the paper says it should appear.
claims.reverse_test: If the pattern can appear without the proposed framework, the claim should be downgraded or narrowed.
claims.evidence_bar: No explicit evidence marker in sentence.
claims.kill_conditions: Needs an explicit failure case: what observation, logic result, or counterexample would make this claim false?
claims.not_claimed: Does not by itself claim that physics proves theology.
claims.proof_boundary: Current boundary: deterministic pass classifies this as Structural Correspondence, not a final proof.
